name: Coding Starter
description: A practical starter blueprint for Python, JavaScript, Git, editor, and optional container workflows.

apps.reason: Required for source control and open-source project workflows.
Python runtime for scripting and backend development.
JavaScript runtime for frontend and tooling workflows.
Recommended package manager for modern JavaScript projects.
Useful for containerized development when the PC has enough resources.
Useful on Windows for Linux-native developer tooling.

config_recommendations: Use one project folder for source code and one virtual environment per Python project.
Prefer LTS Node.js for starter workflows.
Install Docker only if the workflow actually needs containers.
risk_warnings: StackPilot v0.1 does not install developer tools automatically.
Docker can consume significant RAM and disk space on smaller PCs.
not_recommended: Running multiple heavy IDEs, containers, and dev servers at once on low-RAM systems.
next_steps: Install required tools manually from official sources.
Run StackPilot again after installation to verify detected tools.
